problem_statement: Implement synchronous compile-time evaluation for `comptime` blocks in the Civet compiler, modeled after Rust-style comptime: at compile time, evaluate a block of code in an isolated JS realm, serialize the result with JSON (so the value must be acyclic and JSON-serializable), and splice that value into the generated output. Blocks run synchronously and cannot close over outer scopes. Fix `replaceNode` so replacing a node does not return early or corrupt parent links. Fix Predicate typing in pattern matching so the predicate’s first argument is not assumed to have a meaningful type (it may be arbitrary). Wire `comptime` through the parser (HERA), block/declaration/function parsing, traversal, and the playground/unplugin compile APIs as in upstream #1180. Do not add the new tests yet in your first commit.
problem_statement_variant: Add compile-time execution for special blocks so constants can be computed while compiling, with results embedded in the emitted JS/TS; also repair tree rewrite and pattern-match guard typing issues that showed up around the same feature area.
hints: Key new module: source/parser/comptime.civet. Parser integration: source/parser.hera, source/parser/block.civet, source/parser/declaration.civet, source/parser/function.civet, source/parser/lib.civet, source/parser/pattern-matching.civet, source/parser/traversal.civet, source/parser/types.civet, source/parser/util.civet. Helper logic: source/parser/helper.civet. CLI/config: source/cli.civet, source/config.civet, source/main.civet, source/generate.civet, build/esbuild.civet. Failures without the fix surface in test/comptime.civet and the new switch testCase in test/switch.civet.
